routes.title: Birth of Hip-Hop
routes.creator: gpt-5.5
routes.summary: How local parties, DJ technique, Bronx communities, and media circulation turned hip-hop into a global cultural form.
routes.thesis: Hip-hop emerges from DJ practice, community spaces, urban conditions, and media circulation, not from a single point of origin.

routes.title: Disco To Dance Music
routes.creator: gpt-5.5
routes.summary: How New York club spaces, DJs, sound systems, 12-inch mixes, and labels turned disco into modern dance music.
routes.thesis: After its mainstream peak, disco does not disappear; it continues into post-disco, garage, electro, freestyle, and modern remix culture.

routes.title: Punk & New Wave Downtown
routes.creator: gpt-5.5
routes.summary: How downtown New York venues, art spaces, fanzines, and independent labels turned punk into new wave, no wave, and crossover culture.
routes.thesis: Punk and new wave downtown emerged from a dense network of clubs, art scenes, independent media, and labels rather than from one venue alone. CBGB became the central node, but the wider scene moved through glam, no wave, post-punk, dance crossover, and global media circulation.

routes.title: Salsa & Latin New York
routes.creator: gpt-5.5
routes.summary: How Afro-Caribbean rhythm, Puerto Rican and broader Latin migration, labels, clubs, and media made New York a central salsa city.
routes.thesis: Salsa in New York emerged from migration, Afro-Caribbean musical memory, neighborhood dance culture, and a powerful label and concert infrastructure. Fania Records made salsa globally legible, but the route also depends on East Harlem, the South Bronx, Nuyorican cultural spaces, and Latin identity politics.

routes.title: Downtown Experiment / No Wave / Loft Jazz
routes.creator: gpt-5.5
routes.summary: How artist-run rooms, lofts, art spaces, and presenter networks connected free jazz, loft jazz, no wave, and downtown experimental music.
routes.thesis: Downtown experiment in New York grew from musicians and artists building their own rooms, festivals, labels, and performance rules. Loft jazz, no wave, The Kitchen, Roulette, and related scenes show experimental sound as an infrastructure of places, recordings, and cross-disciplinary practice rather than a single style.
